children.heading: Statewide workforce investment activities
children.label: 1 Statewide workforce investment activities
children.text: The Governor shall make the reservation required under .section 3163(a) of this title

children.heading: Statewide rapid response activities
children.label: 2 Statewide rapid response activities
children.text: The Governor shall reserve not more than 25 percent of the total amount allotted to the State under for a fiscal year for statewide rapid response activities described in .section 3172(b)(2)(B) of this titlesection 3174(a)(2)(A) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: the funds that are allotted to the State for adult employment and training activities and statewide workforce investment activities under and are not reserved under subsection (a)(1), in accordance with paragraph (2) or (3); andsection 3172(b)(1)(B) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: the funds that are allotted to the State for dislocated worker employment and training activities and statewide workforce investment activities under and are not reserved under paragraph (1) or (2) of subsection (a), in accordance with paragraph (2).section 3172(b)(2)(B) of this title

children.heading: Adult employment and training activities
children.label: A Adult employment and training activities
I
children.text: 33⅓ percent of the funds on the basis described in ;section 3172(b)(1)(B)(ii)(I) of this title

children.label: II
children.text: 33⅓ percent of the funds on the basis described in ; andsection 3172(b)(1)(B)(ii)(II) of this title

children.label: III
children.text: 33⅓ percent of the funds on the basis described in clauses (ii)(III) and (iii) of .section 3172(b)(1)(B) of this title

children.heading: Minimum percentage
children.label: ii Minimum percentage
children.text: The local area shall not receive an allocation percentage for a fiscal year that is less than 90 percent of the average allocation percentage of the local area for the 2 preceding fiscal years. Amounts necessary for increasing such allocations to local areas to comply with the preceding sentence shall be obtained by ratably reducing the allocations to be made to other local areas under this subparagraph.

children.heading: Definition
children.label: iii Definition
children.text: In this subparagraph, the term “allocation percentage”, used with respect to fiscal year 2015 or a subsequent fiscal year, means a percentage of the funds referred to in clause (i), received through an allocation made under this subparagraph, for the fiscal year. The term, used with respect to fiscal year 2013 or 2014, means a percentage of the amount allocated to local areas under paragraphs (2)(A) and (3) of section 133(b) of the Workforce Investment Act of 1998 [] (as in effect on the day before ), received through an allocation made under paragraph (2)(A) or (3) of that section for fiscal year 2013 or 2014, respectively.29 U.S.C. 2863(b)July 22, 2014

children.heading: Dislocated worker employment and training activities
children.label: B Dislocated worker employment and training activities

children.heading: Allocation
children.label: i Allocation
children.text: In allocating the funds described in paragraph (1)(B) to local areas, a State shall allocate the funds based on an allocation formula prescribed by the Governor of the State. Such formula may be amended by the Governor not more than once for each program year. Such formula shall utilize the most appropriate information available to the Governor to distribute amounts to address the State’s worker readjustment assistance needs.

children.heading: Information
children.label: ii Information
children.text: The information described in clause (i) shall include insured unemployment data, unemployment concentrations, plant closing and mass layoff data, declining industries data, farmer-rancher economic hardship data, and long-term unemployment data.

children.heading: Minimum percentage
children.label: iii Minimum percentage
children.text: The local area shall not receive an allocation percentage for fiscal year 2016 or a subsequent fiscal year that is less than 90 percent of the average allocation percentage of the local area for the 2 preceding fiscal years. Amounts necessary for increasing such allocations to local areas to comply with the preceding sentence shall be obtained by ratably reducing the allocations to be made to other local areas under this subparagraph.

children.heading: Definition
children.label: iv Definition
children.text: In this subparagraph, the term “allocation percentage”, used with respect to fiscal year 2015 or a subsequent fiscal year, means a percentage of the funds referred to in clause (i), received through an allocation made under this subparagraph for the fiscal year. The term, used with respect to fiscal year 2014, means a percentage of the amount allocated to local areas under section 133(b)(2)(B) of the Workforce Investment Act of 1998 [] (as in effect on the day before ), received through an allocation made under that section for fiscal year 2014.29 U.S.C. 2863(b)(2)(B)July 22, 2014

children.label: i
children.text: references in to a State shall be deemed to be references to a local area;section 3172(b) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: references in to all States shall be deemed to be references to all local areas in the State involved; andsection 3172(b) of this title

children.label: iii
children.text: except as described in clause (i), references in to the term “excess number” shall be considered to be references to the term as defined in .section 3172(b)(1) of this titlesection 3172(b)(1) of this title

children.heading: Adult employment and training discretionary allocations
children.label: 3 Adult employment and training discretionary allocations
A
children.text: a portion equal to not less than 70 percent of the funds in accordance with paragraph (2)(A); and

children.label: I
children.text: excess poverty in urban, rural, and suburban local areas; and

children.label: II
children.text: excess unemployment above the State average in urban, rural, and suburban local areas; and

children.label: ii
children.text: was developed by the State board and approved by the Secretary as part of the State plan.

children.label: A
children.text: adult employment and training activities; and

children.label: B
children.text: dislocated worker employment and training activities.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Governor shall allocate the funds described in paragraph (1) to local areas under paragraphs (2) and (3) for the purpose of providing a single system of employment and training activities for adults and dislocated workers in accordance with subsections (c) and (d) of .section 3174 of this title

children.heading: Adults
children.label: i Adults
children.text: Funds allocated under paragraph (2)(A) or (3) shall be used by a local area to contribute to the costs of the one-stop delivery system described in as determined under and to pay for employment and training activities provided to adults in the local area, consistent with .section 3151(e) of this titlesection 3151(h) of this titlesection 3174 of this title

children.heading: Dislocated workers
children.label: ii Dislocated workers
children.text: Funds allocated under paragraph (2)(B) shall be used by a local area to contribute to the costs of the one-stop delivery system described in as determined under and to pay for employment and training activities provided to dislocated workers in the local area, consistent with .section 3151(e) of this titlesection 3151(h) of this titlesection 3174 of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Governor may, in accordance with this subsection and after consultation with the State board, reallocate to eligible local areas within the State amounts that are made available to local areas from allocations made under paragraph (2)(A) or (3) of subsection (b) or a corresponding provision of the Workforce Investment Act of 1998 for adult employment and training activities, or under subsection (b)(2)(B) or a corresponding provision of the Workforce Investment Act of 1998 for dislocated worker employment and training activities (referred to individually in this subsection as a “local allocation”) and that are available for reallocation.

children.label: A
children.text: for adult employment and training activities is equal to the amount by which the unobligated balance of the local allocation under paragraph (2)(A) or (3) of subsection (b) for such activities, at the end of the program year prior to the program year for which the determination under this subparagraph is made, exceeds 20 percent of such allocation for the prior program year; and

children.label: B
children.text: for dislocated worker employment and training activities is equal to the amount by which the unobligated balance of the local allocation under subsection (b)(2)(B) for such activities, at the end of the program year prior to the program year for which the determination under this subparagraph is made, exceeds 20 percent of such allocation for the prior program year.

children.label: A
children.text: with respect to such available amounts that were allocated under paragraph (2)(A) or (3) of subsection (b), an amount based on the relative amount of the local allocation under paragraph (2)(A) or (3) of subsection (b), as appropriate, for the program year for which the determination is made, as compared to the total amount of the local allocations under paragraph (2)(A) or (3) of subsection (b), as appropriate, for all eligible local areas in the State for such program year; and

children.label: B
children.text: with respect to such available amounts that were allocated under subsection (b)(2)(B), an amount based on the relative amount of the local allocation under subsection (b)(2)(B) for the program year for which the determination is made, as compared to the total amount of the local allocations under subsection (b)(2)(B) for all eligible local areas in the State for such program year.

children.label: A
children.text: with respect to funds allocated through a local allocation for adult employment and training activities, a local area that does not have an amount of such funds available for reallocation under paragraph (2) for the program year for which the determination under paragraph (2) is made; and

children.label: B
children.text: with respect to funds allocated through a local allocation for dislocated worker employment and training activities, a local area that does not have an amount of such funds available for reallocation under paragraph (2) for the program year for which the determination under paragraph (2) is made.
